## What's changed

Focus on multi-replica PostgreSQL deployments: Jarvis can now run as a
highly-available deployment with several replicas behind PostgreSQL,
coordinated via leader election, snapshot distribution, and cross-pod WS
fanout — no more single-pod-only operation for production use.

### Added
- **Multi-replica HA on PostgreSQL** — PostgreSQL-backed advisory-lock
  leader election; exactly one pod polls Alertmanager and writes history,
  while every pod keeps serving reads/WS from a snapshot the leader
  distributes via `LISTEN`/`NOTIFY`
- **Cross-pod mutation fanout** — comments, claims, and silences created on
  one pod now broadcast to every other pod's connected WS clients, not just
  the originating pod's own
- **Leader pod label** — the current leader pod is labeled
  `jarvis.kj187.de/role=leader` for at-a-glance visibility via `kubectl get
  pods -L`
- **Helm chart HA surface** — optional `PodDisruptionBudget` and
  `topologySpreadConstraints` passthrough for running `replicaCount > 1`
  against PostgreSQL
- **ServiceMonitor customization** — `metrics.serviceMonitor.relabelings`,
  `metricRelabelings`, `annotations`, and `honorLabels` are now
  configurable in the Helm chart, defaulting to a no-op

### Fixed
- **db:** `RecordStatusChange` is now transactional and advisory-lock
  guarded, closing a race where concurrent writers for the same alert
  episode could produce duplicate history events
- **frontend:** the internal `@claimed-by` filter pseudo-label no longer
  renders as a stray label chip

### Documentation
- New canonical [persistence guide](docs/persistence.md) consolidating
  database backend configuration and multi-replica HA behavior into a
  single source of truth, replacing scattered mentions across other docs

**Full diff:** [v1.8.0...v1.9.0](https://github.com/kj187/jarvis/compare/v1.8.0...v1.9.0)
